findings.description: The 'Background' heading in the PDF is underlined and bold, while in the HTML it appears as a larger heading without underline styling.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Background' is underlined and bold in smaller font. HTML: 'Background' is rendered as a larger section heading without underline.

findings.description: Answer text in the PDF is indented under the question, while in the HTML the answers appear at the same margin level as the questions.
findings.evidence: PDF: ANSWER blocks are visually indented. HTML: ANSWER blocks are flush with QUESTION blocks.

findings.description: In Question 3 answer, the PDF spells it 'ANSWSER' (typo preserved in both), but the indentation formatting differs.
findings.evidence: PDF: indented answer block. HTML: same-level answer block.

findings.description: Answer blocks on page 3 (Questions 10, 11) lose their visual indentation in the HTML compared to the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: Answer text is indented under questions. HTML: Answer text is at the same left margin as question text.

findings.description: Bullet list items in Question 11 answer appear with slightly different formatting but content is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF: bulleted list with bold 'OR'. HTML: bulleted list with bold 'OR' - content matches.

findings.description: Answer blocks on page 4 lose their visual indentation in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: Answers are indented. HTML: Answers are at the same margin level.

findings.description: Answer blocks on page 5 lose their visual indentation in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: Answers for Q17-Q20 are indented. HTML: Answers are at the same margin level as questions.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements in the HTML. The HTML has two nested <main> tags, which is semantically incorrect but does not affect content fidelity.

Several hyperlinks present in the PDF are not preserved as clickable links in the HTML. The PDF contains links to 'Division's website', 'training video', '(available here)', 'US Environmental Protection Agency', 'Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)', and 'Division's website' (for resolutions). The HTML does not include href attributes for most of these.
findings.evidence: PDF has hyperlinks for 'Division's website', 'training video', '(available here)', 'US Environmental Protection Agency', and 'Levee Terms & Definitions (army.mil)'. HTML text mentions these but does not include clickable hyperlinks (no <a> tags with href attributes for most).

findings.description: The typo 'ANSWSER' in Question 3's answer is faithfully preserved from the PDF, which is correct behavior. No meaning-altering text changes were found.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show 'ANSWSER: No.' for Question 3's answer.
